questions.question: In which year did French physicist Sadi Carnot create the Carnot engine, which uses thermodynamic principles to function as an ideal heat engine?
Which of the following physical quantities has zero dimension in mass and time?
What is the relation between the frequency (f) and time period (T) of a sound wave?
What is the value of one carat when expressed in SI units?
What is the specific heat of copper, which is often used for pots and pans because it heats up quickly?
How many mutually independent base quantities are there in the current International System of Units (SI)?
What is the term used for the persistence of sound in a large hall due to repeated reflections from walls and other surfaces?
Which of the following is NOT a state function or state variable in a thermodynamic system?
The luminous efficacy of monochromatic radiation of frequency 540×10¹² Hz, Kcd, is to be ____ when expressed in the unit lumen per watt.
Which law explains the colours of black bodies including the Sun and other stars in terms of their temperatures?
What is the condition for total internal reflection to occur?
questions.option_1: Light must travel from a denser to a rarer medium at an angle greater than the critical angle.
questions.option_2: Light must travel from a rarer medium to a denser medium at an angle of 90°.
questions.option_3: Light must strike the boundary at 90° to the normal separating any two mediums of different refractive indices.
questions.option_4: The refractive indices of both media must be equal.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines impulse in mechanics?
questions.option_1: The product of force and the time for which it acts
questions.option_2: The product of mass and velocity
questions.option_3: The rate of change of momentum multiplied by the distance
questions.option_4: The change in displacement over time

questions.question: What is the value of one roentgen (R) when expressed in SI units?
If we derive an expression for velocity, its dimension should be ________.
How many significant figures are there in 2.308 cm?
Which law is most frequently applied when calculating the electric field, particularly when high symmetry is present?
Which theory suggests that planets were formed out of a cloud of material surrounding a young Sun?
What is the preferred unit used in the scientific literature to express the ratio between two sound intensities?
The process of planet formation through collision and sticking of small celestial bodies is called:
What is the relation between the speed of sound (v), its wavelength (λ) and time period (T)?
Who obtained the first accurate value of the speed of light using a rotating mirror apparatus in 1862?
What is the theoretical value of the Stefan-Boltzmann constant (σ) which defines the power emitted per unit area by a black body as a function of its thermodynamic temperature?
Optical fibres work on the principle of ____________.
What is the function of a diathermic wall in a thermodynamic system?
questions.option_1: It allows the flow of heat between systems.
questions.option_2: It allows the exchange of matter only.
questions.option_3: It prevents the exchange of both matter and energy.
questions.option_4: It allows the flow of only work.

questions.question: What is self-induction?
questions.option_1: Induction of EMF in one coil due to a current in a nearby coil
questions.option_2: Induction of EMF in a coil due to motion in a magnetic field
questions.option_3: Induction of EMF in a coil due to a change in its own current
questions.option_4: Generation of current in a coil due to temperature changes
